calendar.matters:
Petition By Trustee To: Recover Trust Corpus Escheated To Estate & Transfer Trust Property To Court Of Jurisdiction Outside Ca
Off Calendar Jul-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alica Berry for State Controller's Office. Ann Craig appeared by telephone for Carin Snodgras.

Ms. Craig stated that she is not aware of a Power of Attorney signed by Carin. The Court stated that it does not seem the Trustee has the right to assign Trust assets, that they could treat it as Delta Financial being an agent on Carin's behalf and raised the question of what would be the appropriate compensation for Ms. Craig and Delta Financial. Ms. Craig stated that she has nothing to add, that she would be happy to withdraw the request that Delta get anything from what is being distributed and that none of her time is for services to Delta. Ms. Berry stated that the Attorney General does not have a legal interest because it is not a Charitable Trust and that it takes no position. The Petition to Recover Escheated Assets was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Wu. (Not Reported)

Petition By Trustee To: Recover Trust Corpus Escheated To Estate & Transfer Trust Property To Court Of Jurisdiction Outside Ca
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Continued To Jun-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ann Craig appeared by telephone for Carin Snodgras. Alice Berry from the Attorney General's Office ("AG") on behalf of the Controller's Office.

The Court questioned how Carin has authority to execute an assignment over a Trust asset, stated that the property is to be distributed to Gerina in Trust but the petition is requesting that it go to Carin and asked why there is a 20% fee to the investigator when Carin knew all along where the funds were. Ms. Craig stated that she thinks Carin has the right to hire agents to recovery property, that she represents Carin, that Carin's expenses will be paid out of the 20%, that the 20% includes her (Ms. Craig's) fees and that she has an agreement with Delta but that she does not represent them. The Court stated that the question arises under Probate Code 11604 if the Heir Hunter hires and pays an Attorney does that void the agreement. Ms. Craig stated that although Carin knew where the funds were she did not know how to collect them, and that she has a fee agreement with Carin but that she is not sure of the terms. Ms. Berry stated that the Controller's position is that they are comfortable with the Court identifying the Parties entitled to the funds, that they have no position as to the 20% fee, that there is a 5 year statute of limitation once the AG gives notice in Sacramento and that the action has not begun yet. The Petition to Recover Escheated Assets, etc. was continued to June 25, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a declaration from Delta Financial and Ms. Craig specifying who she represents and how her fees are being paid to justify the $40,000.00 in fees to Delta Financial and why it should be 20%.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition By Trustee To: Recover Trust Corpus Escheated To Estate & Transfer Trust Property To Court Of Jurisdiction Outside Ca
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Continued To Jun-11-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Trustee To: Recover Trust Corpus Escheated To Estate & Transfer Trust Property To Court Of Jurisdiction Outside Ca
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Continued To May-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Trustee To: Recover Trust Corpus Escheated To Estate & Transfer Trust Property To Court Of Jurisdiction Outside Ca
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition To Recover Escheated Property Valued Over $60,000.
Off Calendar Feb-02-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ewald Schlachter for Carin Snodgras. Susan Kawala from the State Controller's Office.

The Court denied the petition without prejudice. The Court directed Counsel to file the proper petition to be verified by the Petitioner, not Counsel and stated that proper evidence was needed showing that Gerina (Beneficiary) went by all names.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

PETITION Petition To Recover Escheated Property Valued Over $60,000.
